preamble: The shift from the open-mid back unrounded vowel [ʌ] (the 'strut' or 'caret' vowel) to a more open and central [a] or back [ɑ] is a common feature of several modern English dialects. It represents a lowering of the tongue from the mid-back position.

examples.note: The traditional back vowel has significantly lowered and centralized in modern RP.
In some Southern US and AAVE dialects, the vowel is lowered and backed.
